whyInsufficient: Oxlint has no rule enforcing mechanical naming and banned vague verbs.
Oxlint has no rule for duplicate adjacent naming context in identifiers.
Built-in duplicate import checks do not forbid import-local-then-export indirection.
Built-in restricted type rules cannot ban isRecord helper declarations, calls, and Record-predicate patterns together.
Built-ins do not forbid catch fallback defaults that silently swallow runtime failures.
Oxlint does not provide a runtime-agnostic rule to ban compat/polyfill fallback patterns in strict mode.
Built-in catch rules do not forbid returning caught errors as values, which silently converts failures into success paths.
Oxlint import rules do not validate package.json dependency declaration consistency for imported packages.
Built-ins do not explicitly block chained assertion escapes such as 'as unknown as T'.
Built-ins do not specifically detect self-admitted AI debt comments indicating unverifiable generated code.
Oxlint does not provide a dedicated rule to ban JSON.parse fallback defaults that hide malformed payloads.
Oxlint does not provide a dedicated rule to ban JSON.stringify fallback defaults that hide serialization failures.
Built-in assertion rules do not specifically ban 'as never' escape hatches.
Oxlint has no Effect-specific rule for orDie failure conversion.
Oxlint has no Effect-specific rule requiring catch handler callbacks to preserve and use error context.
Oxlint has no Effect runtime boundary rule for runPromise/runSync execution.
Built-in throw rules do not validate Effect.fail argument error specificity.
Oxlint has no Effect composition style rule for long flatMap chains.
Promise nesting checks do not detect returning Effect values from array iterators.
Oxlint has no span naming convention checks for Effect.fn or Effect.withSpan.
Built-in warning comment rules do not enforce tracked issue references on TODO/FIXME markers.
Oxlint has no rule requiring rationale or ticket references on lint-disable comments.
Oxlint has no rule for banning generic filenames like utils or helpers.
Built-in default export rules cannot scope enforcement to specific architectural layers.
Oxlint has no Effect-specific rule for catching async functions inside Effect.sync.
Oxlint has no Effect runtime rule for preferring runMain in entrypoint files.
Oxlint has no rule for banning generic exported names like processData or handleResult.
Oxlint has no Effect-specific rule for banning point-free usage that hurts inference.
Oxlint has no Effect composition rule restricting Effect.provide to composition roots.
Oxlint has no Effect-specific rule for restricting Layer construction to infrastructure files.
Built-in warning comment rules do not cover string literals, identifiers, or fixture-aware file exclusion.
Oxlint has no rule for detecting vague error messages or generic error-object return patterns.
Oxlint has no Effect rule requiring tagged domain errors in Effect.fail calls.
Built-in any rules cannot scope exceptions to interop adapters or generated code.
Oxlint has no rule limiting inline object type size on exported APIs.
Oxlint has no rule detecting exported types with excessive optional fields and missing discriminants.
Oxlint has no rule enforcing branded types for Id-suffixed type aliases.
Oxlint has no Effect boundary rule for banning Promise returns in service methods.
Built-in throw rules do not restrict naked throw to Effect.try/tryPromise boundaries.
Oxlint has no Effect concurrency rule for ensuring fork results are handled.
Oxlint has no rule for detecting structurally identical if/else or switch branches.
Oxlint has no rule enforcing exhaustive switch on tagged union discriminants.
Oxlint import rules do not detect relative paths crossing workspace package boundaries.
Built-in restricted import rules cannot enforce architectural layer dependency direction.
Oxlint has no rule for detecting near-identical exported function bodies.
